files.src/models.py:
"""Core data types shared across the pipeline."""

from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional


class AlertType(str, Enum):
    WATCH = "WATCH"
    ENTRY = "ENTRY"
    PARTIAL = "PARTIAL"
    CLOSE = "CLOSE"
    UNKNOWN = "UNKNOWN"


class Right(str, Enum):
    CALL = "C"
    PUT = "P"


@dataclass
class ParsedAlert:
    """Structured form of a raw Discord message."""

    type: AlertType
    raw: str
    ticker: Optional[str] = None
    strike: Optional[float] = None
    right: Optional[Right] = None
    alert_price: Optional[float] = None
    confidence: float = 0.0
    notes: str = ""

    def contract_label(self) -> str:
        if self.ticker and self.strike and self.right:
            return f"{self.ticker} {self.strike}{self.right.value}"
        return self.ticker or "?"
files.src/config.py:
"""Configuration with safe defaults. Loads config.yaml if present + PyYAML
is installed; otherwise uses the defaults below so the offline sim just runs.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Optional
import os


@dataclass
class Config:
    trade_budget_usd: float = 300.0
    max_contracts_per_trade: int = 10
    allowlist: List[str] = field(
        default_factory=lambda: ["QQQ", "NVDA", "TSLA", "AAPL", "SPY", "PLTR"])
    # Every exit/risk rule below is OPTIONAL — None/empty means "don't apply it,
    # just follow the alerts" (the trader's own PARTIAL/CLOSE messages still act).
    stop_loss_pct: Optional[float] = 0.20      # resting stop at entry; None = no auto stop
    breakeven_at_pct: Optional[float] = 0.20   # +X% -> move stop to breakeven; None = off
    breakeven_after_partial: bool = True       # after the first partial sell, move the runner's stop to breakeven (off = let it ride)
    take_profit_pct: Optional[float] = None    # +X% -> CLOSE FULLY; None = off
    # Optional TIERED trailing stop, layered ON TOP of the breakeven floor. List of
    # (peak_gain_threshold, give_back_pct), e.g. [(0.30,0.25),(0.50,0.15),(1.0,0.08)]:
    # once peak hits +30% lock in (peak − 25 pts); tighten to 15 pts past +50%, etc.
    # Ratchets up only, never below breakeven. Empty = off.
    trailing_tiers: List[tuple] = field(default_factory=list)
    take_half_at_pct: Optional[float] = 0.50  # +50% -> sell half; None/0 = disabled
    max_hold_minutes: Optional[int] = 30      # auto-close a stuck position; None/0 = off
    # Reject an entry if the live ask is more than this many DOLLARS above the
    # alert's quoted price (wrong-contract guard). None = no guard.
    max_price_slippage_usd: Optional[float] = 0.50
    max_trades_per_day: int = 20
    # Follow ENTRY alerts but IGNORE the trader's PARTIAL/CLOSE alerts — exits come
    # only from this strategy's own rules (stop/breakeven/trailing/targets/EOD).
    ignore_exit_alerts: bool = False
    # Per-weekday budget multiplier in PERCENT. Missing day = 100%. Keyed by lowercase
    # weekday: e.g. {"mon": 50} = half the trade budget on Mondays.
    budget_day_pct: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    kill_switch: bool = False   # hard panic: flatten ALL + block (the top-bar toggle)
    paused: bool = False        # soft: block NEW entries, keep managing open positions
    dry_run: bool = True
    description: str = ""
    # 0DTE end-of-day flatten is HARD-CODED (final backstop), not a tunable param.
    eod_flatten_et: str = "15:58"    # ET, ~2 min before the 16:00 close

    @classmethod
    def load(cls, path: str = "config.yaml") -> "Config":
        if not os.path.exists(path):
            return cls()
        try:
            import yaml  # type: ignore
        except ImportError:
            return cls()
        with open(path) as f:
            data = yaml.safe_load(f) or {}
        known = {k: data[k] for k in data if k in cls.__dataclass_fields__}
        return cls(**known)
files.src/parser.py:
"""Turn a raw Discord alert into a ParsedAlert.

Tuned against nitro_trades' real format:

    Trigger / Entry / 💰 Contract: QQQ $714c / 💵 Price: $2 / 💭Comments:none
    Took out half here on QQQ, SL to b/e on rest
    Closed rest of QQQ nice w
    TSLA $402.5p on watch

Classification is keyword-based with a confidence score. Anything we can't
confidently classify becomes UNKNOWN (confidence 0.0) so the engine SKIPS it
rather than guessing — important given orders auto-fire.
"""

import re
from typing import Iterable, Optional

from .models import AlertType, ParsedAlert, Right

# strike + right, e.g. "$714c", "402.5p", "205 P"
_CONTRACT_RE = re.compile(r"\$?\s*(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*([cp])\b", re.IGNORECASE)
# alert's quoted price, e.g. "Price: $2", "$1.46"
_PRICE_RE = re.compile(r"price[:\s]*\$?\s*(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)", re.IGNORECASE)

# Partial-sell phrasings. The trader is loose here ("ha;f" typo seen in the
# wild), so ha.?f matches half / ha;f / haf. NOTE: "closed half" is a TRIM, not
# a full close, so it must be caught here (partial is checked before close).
_PARTIAL_PATTERNS = [
    r"took\s+out\s+ha.?f",
    r"took\s+ha.?f",
    r"sold\s+ha.?f",
    r"clos\w*\s+ha.?f",   # "closed half of NVDA" -> trim, NOT a full close
    r"ha.?f\s+here",
    r"\btrim",
    r"took\s+some",
    r"scal(?:e|ed|ing)\s+out",
    r"locked\s+in",       # "LOCKED IN MAJORITY PROFIT" = a scale (position-aware)
    r"\bmajorit",         # "took majority", "locked in majority"
]
_PARTIAL_RE = re.compile("|".join(_PARTIAL_PATTERNS), re.IGNORECASE)

# Full take-profit / runner exit -> CLOSE. Checked BEFORE partial so "locked in
# fully" isn't caught by the "locked in" partial rule. ("locked in majority"
# stays a partial.)
_FULL_TP_RE = re.compile(r"full\s*tp|locked\s+in\b[^.\n]{0,25}\bfull", re.IGNORECASE)

# Uppercase tokens that look like tickers but aren't.
_STOPWORDS = {"SL", "B", "E", "BE", "HIGH", "RISK", "NONE", "TP", "DTE", "W", "L"}


def _find_ticker(text: str, allowlist: Optional[Iterable[str]]) -> Optional[str]:
    candidates = re.findall(r"\b([A-Z]{1,5})\b", text)
    if allowlist:
        allow = {t.upper() for t in allowlist}
        for tok in candidates:
            if tok in allow:
                return tok
    for tok in candidates:
        if tok not in _STOPWORDS:
            return tok
    return None


def _find_contract(text: str):
    m = _CONTRACT_RE.search(text)
    if not m:
        return None, None
    strike = float(m.group(1))
    right = Right.CALL if m.group(2).upper() == "C" else Right.PUT
    return strike, right


def parse(raw: str, allowlist: Optional[Iterable[str]] = None) -> ParsedAlert:
    text = raw.strip()
    low = text.lower()

    # --- WATCH: pre-stage a contract, no order ---
    # Standard "TICKER $strike on watch", plus the "Watching TICKER $strike ..."
    # variant — but only when an actual contract is present, so chatter like
    # "watching the open" doesn't trigger.
    strike, right = _find_contract(text)
    if "on watch" in low or ("watch" in low and strike and right):
        ticker = _find_ticker(text, allowlist)
        ok = bool(ticker and strike and right)
        return ParsedAlert(
            type=AlertType.WATCH,
            raw=raw,
            ticker=ticker,
            strike=strike,
            right=right,
            confidence=0.9 if ok else 0.3,
            notes="" if ok else "watch missing ticker/strike/right",
        )

    # --- ENTRY: explicit Entry/Contract line ---
    if "entry" in low or "contract:" in low:
        strike, right = _find_contract(text)
        ticker = _find_ticker(text, allowlist)
        price_m = _PRICE_RE.search(text)
        alert_price = float(price_m.group(1)) if price_m else None
        ok = bool(ticker and strike and right)
        return ParsedAlert(
            type=AlertType.ENTRY,
            raw=raw,
            ticker=ticker,
            strike=strike,
            right=right,
            alert_price=alert_price,
            confidence=0.9 if ok else 0.2,
            notes="" if ok else "entry missing ticker/strike/right",
        )

    # --- Full take-profit / "locked in ... fully" / "full TP" = full CLOSE ---
    # (before PARTIAL so the "locked in" partial rule doesn't grab the fully case)
    if _FULL_TP_RE.search(text):
        ticker = _find_ticker(text, allowlist)
        return ParsedAlert(
            type=AlertType.CLOSE,
            raw=raw,
            ticker=ticker,
            strike=strike, right=right,   # contract (if named) -> disambiguates multi-open
            confidence=0.8 if ticker else 0.3,
            notes="full take-profit" if ticker else "full-TP missing ticker",
        )

    # --- PARTIAL before CLOSE: "took out half ... on rest" is a trim, not a close ---
    if _PARTIAL_RE.search(text):
        ticker = _find_ticker(text, allowlist)
        return ParsedAlert(
            type=AlertType.PARTIAL,
            raw=raw,
            ticker=ticker,
            strike=strike, right=right,   # contract (if named) -> disambiguates multi-open
            confidence=0.85 if ticker else 0.3,
            notes="" if ticker else "partial missing ticker",
        )

    # --- CLOSE (incl. "about to close" / "bouta close runners soon" = full close) ---
    if "clos" in low or low.startswith("out ") or " out of " in low:
        ticker = _find_ticker(text, allowlist)
        return ParsedAlert(
            type=AlertType.CLOSE,
            raw=raw,
            ticker=ticker,
            strike=strike, right=right,   # contract (if named) -> disambiguates multi-open
            confidence=0.85 if ticker else 0.3,
            notes="" if ticker else "close missing ticker",
        )

    return ParsedAlert(type=AlertType.UNKNOWN, raw=raw, confidence=0.0,
                       notes="unrecognized alert")
files.src/positions.py:
"""Track open option positions, keyed by underlying ticker.

Alerts reference sells by ticker only ("took half on QQQ"), so we map a ticker
to the single contract currently open on it. One position per ticker at a time,
which matches how the alerts read.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Dict, Optional

from .models import Right


@dataclass
class Position:
    ticker: str
    osi_symbol: str
    right: Right
    strike: float
    quantity: int
    fill_price: float
    stop_order_id: Optional[str] = None
    stop_price: Optional[float] = None  # current stop level (for dry-run stop sim)
    half_sold: bool = False  # True once a +50% or partial trim has fired
    stop_at_breakeven: bool = False  # True once the stop has been moved to fill price
    rec_id: Optional[int] = None  # recorder position id (for the DB)
    realized: float = 0.0  # running realized P&L for this position (dollars)
    opened_at: Optional[datetime] = None  # entry time (for max-hold auto-close)
    last_pushed_price: Optional[float] = None  # last live price mirrored to the DB
    high_water: float = 0.0  # highest contract price seen (for the trailing stop)


class PositionBook:
    def __init__(self):
        self._by_ticker: Dict[str, Position] = {}

    def open(self, pos: Position) -> None:
        self._by_ticker[pos.ticker] = pos

    def get(self, ticker: str) -> Optional[Position]:
        return self._by_ticker.get(ticker)

    def close(self, ticker: str) -> None:
        self._by_ticker.pop(ticker, None)

    def all(self):
        return list(self._by_ticker.values())
files.src/schwab/symbols.py:
"""Build OSI option symbols and pick the 0DTE expiry.

OSI is the 21-char format Schwab expects in an order's instrument.symbol:

    Root (6, left-justified, space-padded) + YYMMDD + C/P + strike*1000 (8 digits)

    QQQ  714C exp 2026-06-10 -> "QQQ   260610C00714000"
    TSLA 402.5P exp 2026-06-10 -> "TSLA  260610P00402500"
"""

from datetime import date, timedelta

from ..models import Right


def next_trading_day(today: date) -> date:
    """0DTE = today, unless it's a weekend or NYSE holiday -> next trading day."""
    from .market_calendar import is_trading_day
    d = today
    while not is_trading_day(d):
        d += timedelta(days=1)
    return d


def build_osi(ticker: str, expiry: date, right: Right, strike: float) -> str:
    root = ticker.upper().ljust(6)
    ymd = expiry.strftime("%y%m%d")
    strike_int = int(round(strike * 1000))
    return f"{root}{ymd}{right.value}{strike_int:08d}"
files.src/engine.py:
"""Orchestrates the pipeline: parsed alert -> rules -> orders -> position book.

handle_alert() is the single entry point the Discord watcher will call per
message. check_targets() is the +50% monitor, called on a timer while running.
"""

from __future__ import annotations   # all annotations become lazy strings (no runtime eval)

from datetime import date, datetime, timedelta
from math import ceil
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Optional

from . import parser
from .config import Config
from .models import AlertType, ParsedAlert
from .paper import PaperLedger
from .positions import Position, PositionBook
from .rules import RulesEngine
from .schwab import orders
from .schwab.symbols import build_osi, next_trading_day

if TYPE_CHECKING:   # type hints only — NOT imported at runtime, so the engine (and its
    from .quotes import QuoteProvider          # exit logic) loads without `requests`,
    from .schwab.client import SchwabClient    # e.g. inside Pyodide for the replay


class TradingEngine:
    def __init__(self, config: Config, client: SchwabClient,
                 quotes: QuoteProvider, today: Optional[date] = None,
                 fallback_parser=None, resolver=None, recorder=None,
                 strategy_id=None, strategy_label=None):
        self.config = config
        self.client = client
        self.quotes = quotes
        # strategy_id/label: set when this engine is one of several fanned out from
        # the strategies table; None in single-engine/legacy mode. Tags recorded rows.
        self.strategy_id = strategy_id
        self.strategy_label = strategy_label
        self.rules = RulesEngine(config)
        self.book = PositionBook()
        self.ledger = PaperLedger()  # simulated fills + P&L (populated in dry-run)
        self._today = today
        # Optional callable(raw, allowlist) -> ParsedAlert, tried when the
        # keyword parser is unsure. See src/llm_parser.parse_with_llm.
        self._fallback_parser = fallback_parser
        # Optional SchwabChainResolver: resolve(ticker, right, strike) -> symbol.
        # When None (tests/offline), fall back to build_osi + assumed 0DTE.
        self._resolver = resolver
        self.recorder = recorder  # optional Recorder (persists everything seen/done)
        self._watched: dict[str, str] = {}  # ticker -> osi symbol staged on watch
        # Fronttest tape: keep sampling each entered contract's price into a
        # SEPARATE store (fronttest_tape) from entry until 30 min AFTER the live
        # trade closes, so alternative EXIT strategies can be replayed over the
        # full path. {rec_id: {"symbol": osi, "close_at": datetime|None}}.
        self._fronttest: dict = {}
        self.fronttest_tape_minutes = 30

    def _resolve(self, ticker, right, strike) -> Optional[str]:
        """Real listed Schwab symbol via the chain resolver (instant, cached);
        falls back to assembling a 0DTE symbol when no resolver is set."""
        if self._resolver is not None:
            return self._resolver.resolve(ticker, right, strike)
        return build_osi(ticker, self._expiry(), right, strike)

    def _expiry(self) -> date:
        base = self._today or date.today()
        return next_trading_day(base)

    def handle_alert(self, raw: str, discord_ts=None, source_id=None) -> None:
        """Single-engine entry point: parse + record + act. The multi-engine fan-out
        (src/ingest.py) parses + records ONCE and calls handle_parsed() per engine."""
        # discord_ts = original Discord post time (datetime, optional). Stored so the
        # dashboard can show the Discord->order delay; never gates any decision.
        alert = parser.parse(raw, allowlist=self.config.allowlist)

        # If the keyword parser is unsure, ask the LLM fallback (if configured)
        # and take its answer when it's more confident.
        if alert.confidence < 0.6 and self._fallback_parser is not None:
            llm = self._fallback_parser(raw, self.config.allowlist)
            if llm is not None and llm.confidence > alert.confidence:
                print(f"    (llm fallback: {alert.confidence:.2f} -> {llm.confidence:.2f})")
                alert = llm

        print(f"\n» {alert.type.value:7} | {alert.contract_label():14} "
              f"| conf {alert.confidence:.2f} | {raw.strip().splitlines()[-1][:50]}")
        if alert.notes:
            print(f"    note: {alert.notes}")

        if self.recorder:
            fired = alert.type in (AlertType.WATCH, AlertType.ENTRY,
                                   AlertType.PARTIAL, AlertType.CLOSE)
            self.recorder.log_alert(raw.strip(), alert.type.value, alert.ticker,
                                    fired, alert.notes, discord_ts=discord_ts,
                                    source_id=source_id)
        self.handle_parsed(alert)

    def handle_parsed(self, alert: ParsedAlert) -> None:
        """Act on an already-parsed alert. Used by the fan-out (no re-parse/re-log)
        and by handle_alert. Per-strategy allowlist/sizing still apply in the handlers."""
        if self.strategy_label:
            print(f"    [{self.strategy_label}]")
        handler = {
            AlertType.WATCH: self._on_watch,
            AlertType.ENTRY: self._on_entry,
            AlertType.PARTIAL: self._on_partial,
            AlertType.CLOSE: self._on_close,
        }.get(alert.type)

        if handler is None:
            print("    SKIP: unrecognized alert")
            return
        handler(alert)

    # ----- handlers -----

    def _on_watch(self, a: ParsedAlert) -> None:
        if not (a.ticker and a.strike and a.right):
            print("    SKIP: incomplete watch alert")
            return
        symbol = self._resolve(a.ticker, a.right, a.strike)
        if not symbol:
            print(f"    SKIP: no listed {a.ticker} {a.strike}{a.right.value} contract")
            return
        self._watched[a.ticker] = symbol
        print(f"    STAGED: {symbol} — ready for entry")

    def _on_entry(self, a: ParsedAlert) -> None:
        if not (a.ticker and a.strike and a.right):
            print("    SKIP: incomplete entry alert")
            return
        symbol = self._resolve(a.ticker, a.right, a.strike)
        if not symbol:
            print(f"    REJECT: no listed {a.ticker} {a.strike}{a.right.value} contract")
            return
        ask = self.quotes.ask(symbol)  # live quote for sizing — must be fresh

        decision = self.rules.validate_entry(a, ask)
        if not decision.approved:
            print(f"    REJECT: {decision.reason}")
            return
        print(f"    OK: {decision.reason}")

        self.client.place_order(orders.market_buy(symbol, decision.quantity),
                                label="BUY")
        self.rules.record_trade()

        # Simulate the fill at the ask for the resting stop (live: use real fill).
        fill = ask
        if self.client.dry_run:
            self.ledger.buy(a.ticker, decision.quantity, fill)
        pos = Position(
            ticker=a.ticker, osi_symbol=symbol, right=a.right, strike=a.strike,
            quantity=decision.quantity, fill_price=fill, opened_at=datetime.now(),
            high_water=fill)
        self.book.open(pos)
        if self.recorder:
            pos.rec_id = self.recorder.open_position(
                a.ticker, symbol, a.right.value, a.strike, decision.quantity, fill,
                strategy_id=self.strategy_id)
            self.recorder.log_event(pos.rec_id, "entry", decision.quantity, fill)
            self._fronttest[pos.rec_id] = {"symbol": symbol, "close_at": None}  # start the tape
            self.recorder.log_tape(pos.rec_id, fill)
        if self.config.stop_loss_pct is not None:   # None = no auto stop (follow alerts)
            self._set_stop(pos, fill * (1 - self.config.stop_loss_pct),
                           f"-{int(self.config.stop_loss_pct*100)}%")

    def _resolve_for_manage(self, a: ParsedAlert):
        """Find the open position a PARTIAL/CLOSE refers to. In order:
          1. exact ticker we hold;
          2. names a REAL allowlisted ticker we DON'T hold -> None (another symbol);
          3. tickerless but carries a contract -> the lone open position whose
             strike+right match;
          4. a single open position -> it (handles junk tickers like 'IN');
          5. 2+ open and nothing distinguishes them -> apply to the HIGHEST-GAIN
             open trade (the trader scales winners) AND raise a loud, persisted
             operator flag naming the trade(s) left unmanaged."""
        t = (a.ticker or "").upper()
        if t:
            pos = self.book.get(t)
            if pos:
                return pos
            if t in {x.upper() for x in self.config.allowlist}:
                return None  # names a real ticker we're not in -> not ours
        opens = self.book.all()
        if not opens:
            return None
        if len(opens) == 1:
            return opens[0]
        if a.strike is not None and a.right is not None:        # 3) contract match
            matches = [p for p in opens if p.strike == a.strike and p.right == a.right]
            if len(matches) == 1:
                return matches[0]
        chosen = max(opens, key=self._unrealized_gain)          # 5) winner + flag
        self._flag_ambiguous(a, chosen, opens)
        return chosen

    def _unrealized_gain(self, pos: Position) -> float:
        last = self.quotes.last(pos.osi_symbol) or pos.last_pushed_price or pos.fill_price
        return (last / pos.fill_price - 1) if pos.fill_price else 0.0

    def _flag_ambiguous(self, a: ParsedAlert, chosen: Position, opens) -> None:
        """2+ open trades and an alert we can't tie to one: we acted on the winner,
        but the operator must know the others were left untouched."""
        others = [p.ticker for p in opens if p is not chosen]
        g = self._unrealized_gain(chosen) * 100
        msg = (f"Ambiguous {a.type.value} (no ticker/contract in alert): acted on the "
               f"most-profitable trade {chosen.ticker} ({g:+.0f}%). Left open / NOT "
               f"managed by this alert — review manually: {', '.join(others)}")
        print(f"    ⚠️  {msg}")
        if self.recorder:
            self.recorder.log_flag("ambiguous_alert", msg, ticker=chosen.ticker,
                                   strategy_id=self.strategy_id)

    def _on_partial(self, a: ParsedAlert) -> None:
        if self.config.ignore_exit_alerts:
            print("    PARTIAL ignored — this strategy manages exits by its own rules")
            return
        pos = self._resolve_for_manage(a)
        if not pos:
            print(f"    SKIP: no matching open position (alert ticker {a.ticker!r})")
            return
        # A scale signal (e.g. "LOCKED IN MAJORITY") takes half ONCE to lock in profit.
        # If we've ALREADY trimmed, we DON'T force-close the runner any more — it rides
        # its stop (trailing / breakeven) so the strategy's exit logic decides. A real
        # "closed" alert still closes fully (via _on_close).
        if pos.half_sold:
            print("    scale alert — runner already trimmed; it rides its stop (not force-closed)")
            return
        if pos.quantity <= 1:
            print("    scale alert on a single contract -> closing fully (can't trim)")
            self._exit(pos, pos.quantity, note="scale alert (1 contract)")
            return
        # Round the sold amount UP — profit preservation (3 contracts -> sell 2).
        self._trim(pos, ceil(pos.quantity / 2), note="partial alert")

    def _on_close(self, a: ParsedAlert) -> None:
        if self.config.ignore_exit_alerts:
            print("    CLOSE ignored — this strategy manages exits by its own rules")
            return
        pos = self._resolve_for_manage(a)
        if not pos:
            print(f"    SKIP: no matching open position (alert ticker {a.ticker!r})")
            return
        self._exit(pos, pos.quantity, note="close alert")

    # ----- order helpers -----

    def _set_stop(self, pos: Position, stop_price: float, reason: str) -> None:
        """Cancel any existing stop and place a fresh one for pos.quantity."""
        stop_price = round(stop_price, 2)
        if pos.stop_order_id:
            self.client.cancel_order(pos.stop_order_id)
        pos.stop_order_id = self.client.place_order(
            orders.stop_sell(pos.osi_symbol, pos.quantity, stop_price),
            label=f"STOP {reason}")
        pos.stop_price = stop_price
        pos.stop_at_breakeven = abs(stop_price - round(pos.fill_price, 2)) < 1e-9
        if self.recorder:
            self.recorder.log_event(pos.rec_id, "stop_set", pos.quantity,
                                    stop_price, note=reason)
            # Mirror the live stop level onto the position row for the dashboard.
            self.recorder.update_position(pos.rec_id, stop_price=stop_price)

    def _move_stop_to_breakeven(self, pos: Position) -> None:
        if pos.stop_at_breakeven:
            return
        self._set_stop(pos, pos.fill_price, "→ breakeven")
        print(f"    {pos.ticker} stop -> breakeven (${pos.fill_price:.2f})")

    def _trim(self, pos: Position, qty: int, rule: bool = False, note: str = "") -> None:
        # Sells fill at the BID (what you actually get), not last/mid.
        sell_price = self.quotes.bid(pos.osi_symbol) or self.quotes.last(pos.osi_symbol) or pos.fill_price
        self.client.place_order(orders.market_sell(pos.osi_symbol, qty), label="TRIM")
        if self.client.dry_run:
            self.ledger.sell(pos.ticker, qty, sell_price, "trim")
        pos.realized += (sell_price - pos.fill_price) * 100 * qty
        pos.quantity -= qty
        pos.half_sold = True
        if self.recorder:
            # rule take-half ("take_half") vs trader's partial alert ("trim") — so the
            # dashboard log shows the true trigger (gear vs bell), with a reason note.
            self.recorder.log_event(pos.rec_id, "take_half" if rule else "trim", qty, sell_price, note=note)
            # Mutate the position row so the dashboard shows the partial live:
            # remaining contracts, running realized P&L, and the ½-sold flag.
            self.recorder.update_position(pos.rec_id, qty=pos.quantity,
                                          realized_pnl=round(pos.realized, 2),
                                          half_sold=1)
        print(f"    sold {qty} @ ~${sell_price:.2f}, {pos.quantity} remaining")
        # First sell done -> move the runner's stop to breakeven ONLY if the strategy
        # opts into it. Off = "let the runner ride" (managed by its trailing / initial
        # stop). This is its own option, separate from the +X% breakeven rule.
        if getattr(self.config, "breakeven_after_partial", True):
            self._set_stop(pos, pos.fill_price, "→ breakeven (after sell)")

    def _exit(self, pos: Position, qty: int, reason: str = "close",
              fill_price: Optional[float] = None, note: str = "") -> None:
        # fill_price set (e.g. stop) -> use it; otherwise fill at the BID.
        sell_price = fill_price or self.quotes.bid(pos.osi_symbol) or self.quotes.last(pos.osi_symbol) or pos.fill_price
        self.client.place_order(orders.market_sell(pos.osi_symbol, qty), label="CLOSE")
        if self.client.dry_run:
            self.ledger.sell(pos.ticker, qty, sell_price, reason)
        pos.realized += (sell_price - pos.fill_price) * 100 * qty
        if self.recorder:
            self.recorder.log_event(pos.rec_id, reason, qty, sell_price, note=note)
            self.recorder.close_position(pos.rec_id, sell_price, pos.realized)
            # keep the fronttest tape running for N min after the live close
            ft = self._fronttest.get(pos.rec_id)
            if ft is not None:
                ft["close_at"] = datetime.now() + timedelta(minutes=self.fronttest_tape_minutes)
        if pos.stop_order_id:
            self.client.cancel_order(pos.stop_order_id)
        self.book.close(pos.ticker)
        print(f"    closed {pos.ticker} @ ~${sell_price:.2f} ({reason})")

    # ----- price-driven monitor (call on a timer while running) -----

    def check_targets(self) -> None:
        """Two profit triggers, checked on every poll tick:
          +50% (no sell yet)  -> sell half  (which also moves the stop to breakeven)
          +20%                -> move the stop to breakeven (no sell)
        """
        for pos in self.book.all():
            q = self.quotes.quote(pos.osi_symbol)   # one fetch -> last+bid+ask
            last = q.get("last") or 0.0
            bid = q.get("bid") or None
            ask = q.get("ask") or None
            if last <= 0:
                continue
            if self.recorder:
                # last drives decisions; bid/ask captured so replays fill realistically
                self.recorder.log_sample(pos.rec_id, last, bid=bid, ask=ask)
                self.recorder.log_tape(pos.rec_id, last, bid=bid, ask=ask)
                # Mirror the live mark onto the position row (only when it moves)
                # so the dashboard's P&L $/% tick in real time. Async, never blocks.
                if last != pos.last_pushed_price:
                    pos.last_pushed_price = last
                    self.recorder.update_position(pos.rec_id, last_price=last)
            # Dry-run only: simulate the resting stop firing (live, Schwab does it).
            # Trigger on last (the mark), but fill at the bid — what you'd really get.
            if (self.client.dry_run and pos.stop_price
                    and last <= pos.stop_price):
                print(f"\n» STOP HIT {pos.ticker} (last ${last:.2f} <= "
                      f"stop ${pos.stop_price:.2f})")
                self._exit(pos, pos.quantity, reason="stop",
                           fill_price=(bid or pos.stop_price))
                continue
            if last > pos.high_water:        # drives the trailing stop
                pos.high_water = last
            gain = last / pos.fill_price - 1

            # Take profit: close the WHOLE position at +take_profit_pct (None = off).
            tp = self.config.take_profit_pct
            if tp and gain >= tp - 1e-9:
                print(f"\n» TAKE PROFIT +{int(tp*100)}% on {pos.ticker} "
                      f"(last ${last:.2f}) — closing fully")
                self._exit(pos, pos.quantity, reason="take_profit", note=f"+{int(tp*100)}% rule")
                continue

            th = self.config.take_half_at_pct  # None / 0 => take-half disabled
            if th and not pos.half_sold and gain >= th:
                if pos.quantity > 1:
                    print(f"\n» TARGET +{int(th*100)}% on {pos.ticker} "
                          f"(last ${last:.2f} vs fill ${pos.fill_price:.2f}) — sell half")
                    self._trim(pos, ceil(pos.quantity / 2), rule=True, note=f"+{int(th*100)}% rule")
                else:
                    print(f"\n» TARGET +{int(th*100)}% on {pos.ticker} "
                          f"(1 contract — closing fully)")
                    self._exit(pos, pos.quantity, reason="target", note=f"+{int(th*100)}% rule")

            # ----- stop management (skip if the take-half just closed the position) -----
            if self.book.get(pos.ticker) is not pos:
                continue
            # 1) Breakeven FLOOR — once past +breakeven_at_pct, lift the stop to
            #    breakeven. Only ever RAISES (so a later trail above it isn't undone).
            be = self.config.breakeven_at_pct   # None = no breakeven move
            if (be is not None and gain >= be - 1e-9
                    and (pos.stop_price is None
                         or pos.stop_price < round(pos.fill_price, 2) - 1e-9)):
                print(f"\n» TARGET +{int(be*100)}% on "
                      f"{pos.ticker} (last ${last:.2f}) — stop to breakeven")
                self._move_stop_to_breakeven(pos)
            # 2) Tiered trailing ON TOP — trail the peak, tightening tier by tier.
            tiers = self.config.trailing_tiers  # [] => off (breakeven floor only)
            if tiers:
                peak_gain = pos.high_water / pos.fill_price - 1
                trail = None
                for at_gain, trail_pct in tiers:   # ascending -> last match = tightest
                    if peak_gain >= at_gain - 1e-9:
                        trail = trail_pct
                if trail is not None:
                    # Lock in (peak profit − trail) POINTS of profit: give back at most
                    # `trail` points from the peak. stop = entry × (1 + peak_gain − trail)
                    #       = high_water − trail × entry.
                    # Ratchet up only; never below the breakeven floor.
                    cand = round(pos.high_water - trail * pos.fill_price, 2)
                    floor = round(pos.fill_price, 2) if (be is not None and peak_gain >= be - 1e-9) else 0.0
                    cand = max(cand, floor)
                    if pos.stop_price is None or cand >= pos.stop_price + 0.02:
                        locked = round((cand / pos.fill_price - 1) * 100)
                        print(f"\n» TRAIL {pos.ticker}: stop -> ${cand:.2f} (+{locked}% locked) "
                              f"(peak +{int(round(peak_gain*100))}%, give back {int(round(trail*100))}%)")
                        self._set_stop(pos, cand, f"trail {int(round(trail*100))}%")

    def check_tape(self, now: Optional[datetime] = None) -> None:
        """Fronttest tape: keep sampling each CLOSED contract's price into
        fronttest_tape until `fronttest_tape_minutes` after its live close, then
        stop. Open positions are taped by check_targets, so here we only handle
        the post-close window. Call on every poll tick."""
        if not self.recorder or not self._fronttest:
            return
        now = now or datetime.now()
        done = []
        for rec_id, ft in self._fronttest.items():
            close_at = ft.get("close_at")
            if close_at is None:
                continue              # still open -> check_targets is taping it
            if now >= close_at:
                done.append(rec_id)   # window elapsed -> stop taping
                continue
            q = self.quotes.quote(ft["symbol"])
            last = q.get("last") or 0.0
            if last and last > 0:
                self.recorder.log_tape(rec_id, last,
                                       bid=(q.get("bid") or None), ask=(q.get("ask") or None))
        for rec_id in done:
            self._fronttest.pop(rec_id, None)

    def check_kill_switch(self) -> None:
        """Operator kill switch (tripped from the dashboard): the instant it's on,
        market-close EVERY open position. New entries are already blocked by the
        rules engine while kill_switch is set. Safe to call on every poll tick."""
        if not self.config.kill_switch:
            return
        positions = self.book.all()
        if not positions:
            return
        print(f"\n» KILL SWITCH ENGAGED: market-closing {len(positions)} open "
              f"position(s) now")
        for pos in positions:
            self._exit(pos, pos.quantity, reason="kill")

    def check_eod_flatten(self, now_et: Optional[datetime] = None) -> None:
        """0DTE safety: market-close every open position at/after eod_flatten_et
        (ET) so nothing expires or auto-exercises. Call on every poll tick."""
        positions = self.book.all()
        if not positions:
            return
        if now_et is None:
            try:
                from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo
                now_et = datetime.now(ZoneInfo("America/New_York"))
            except Exception:
                return  # can't determine ET reliably -> don't guess
        hh, mm = (int(x) for x in self.config.eod_flatten_et.split(":"))
        if (now_et.hour, now_et.minute) >= (hh, mm):
            print(f"\n» EOD FLATTEN ({self.config.eod_flatten_et} ET): closing "
                  f"{len(positions)} open position(s) before the bell")
            for pos in positions:
                self._exit(pos, pos.quantity, reason="eod")

    def check_max_hold(self, now: Optional[datetime] = None) -> None:
        """Auto-close any position open longer than max_hold_minutes — catches a
        missed close / stuck trade fast (these are seconds-to-minutes scalps).
        Disabled when max_hold_minutes is None/0."""
        mh = self.config.max_hold_minutes
        if not mh:
            return
        now = now or datetime.now()
        for pos in self.book.all():
            if pos.opened_at and (now - pos.opened_at).total_seconds() >= mh * 60:
                print(f"\n» MAX HOLD {mh}m exceeded on {pos.ticker} — closing")
                self._exit(pos, pos.quantity, reason="max_hold")

    def session_summary(self) -> str:
        last = {}
        for p in self.book.all():
            lp = self.quotes.last(p.osi_symbol)
            if lp:
                last[p.ticker] = lp
        return self.ledger.summary(last)
files.src/remote_config.py:
"""Live strategy config from Supabase (the dashboard's `strategy_params` row).

The bot is controlled live from reezer.io: it loads params from Supabase at
startup and re-checks them every few seconds while running, mutating the live
Config in place so changes (budget, stops, allowlist, kill switch, dry-run…)
take effect within seconds — no restart. Reads use the bot's service_role key,
which bypasses RLS. Falls back to config.yaml / defaults if Supabase is down.
"""

import os
from typing import Optional, Tuple

from .config import Config


def _headers():
    k = os.environ.get("SUPABASE_KEY", "")
    return {"apikey": k, "Authorization": "Bearer " + k}


def fetch_row() -> Optional[dict]:
    url = os.environ.get("SUPABASE_URL")
    if not url or not os.environ.get("SUPABASE_KEY"):
        return None
    import requests
    r = requests.get(url + "/rest/v1/strategy_params?id=eq.1&limit=1",
                     headers=_headers(), timeout=8)
    r.raise_for_status()
    data = r.json()
    return data[0] if data else None


def _parse_tiers(v) -> list:
    """Parse the trailing_tiers jsonb -> sorted list of (peak_gain, trail_pct) tuples.
    Accepts [[at,trail],...] or [{"at":..,"trail":..},...]; ignores malformed entries."""
    out = []
    if isinstance(v, list):
        for item in v:
            try:
                if isinstance(item, dict):
                    at, tr = float(item.get("at")), float(item.get("trail"))
                else:
                    at, tr = float(item[0]), float(item[1])
                if at > 0 and tr > 0:
                    out.append((at, tr))
            except Exception:
                pass
    out.sort(key=lambda t: t[0])
    return out


def row_to_kwargs(row: dict) -> dict:
    """Map a strategy_params row -> Config field overrides."""
    allow = [s.strip().upper() for s in (row.get("allowlist") or "").split(",") if s.strip()]
    opt_f = lambda v: None if v is None else float(v)
    opt_i = lambda v: None if v is None else int(v)
    kw = {
        "trade_budget_usd": float(row.get("trade_budget_usd") or 0),
        "max_contracts_per_trade": int(row.get("max_contracts_per_trade") or 10),
        "allowlist": allow or ["QQQ"],  # never empty
        # exits/risk are all OPTIONAL — null in the DB means "follow the alerts"
        "stop_loss_pct": opt_f(row.get("stop_loss_pct")),
        "breakeven_at_pct": opt_f(row.get("breakeven_at_pct")),
        "breakeven_after_partial": row.get("breakeven_after_partial") is not False,
        "take_profit_pct": opt_f(row.get("take_profit_pct")),
        "take_half_at_pct": opt_f(row.get("take_half_at_pct")),
        "trailing_tiers": _parse_tiers(row.get("trailing_tiers")),
        "max_hold_minutes": opt_i(row.get("max_hold_minutes")),
        "max_price_slippage_usd": opt_f(row.get("max_price_slippage_usd")),
        "max_trades_per_day": int(row.get("max_trades_per_day") or 999999),
        "ignore_exit_alerts": bool(row.get("ignore_exit_alerts")),
        "budget_day_pct": row.get("budget_day_pct") or {},
        "kill_switch": bool(row.get("kill_switch")),
        "paused": bool(row.get("paused")),
        # dry_run defaults SAFE: only an explicit False switches to live trading
        "dry_run": row.get("dry_run") is not False,
    }
    return kw


def load_config() -> Tuple[Config, Optional[dict]]:
    """Config from Supabase if reachable, else config.yaml / defaults."""
    cfg = Config.load()
    try:
        row = fetch_row()
        if row:
            for k, v in row_to_kwargs(row).items():
                setattr(cfg, k, v)
            print("Config: loaded live from Supabase (Reezer strategy_params)")
            return cfg, row
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Config: Supabase load failed ({e}); using local config")
    return cfg, None


def refresh_into(config: Config, client=None) -> list:
    """Re-fetch and apply changes to the live Config in place. Returns the list
    of changed field names (empty = no change)."""
    try:
        row = fetch_row()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"    [config] refresh failed: {e}")
        return []
    if not row:
        return []
    changed = []
    for k, v in row_to_kwargs(row).items():
        if getattr(config, k, None) != v:
            setattr(config, k, v)
            changed.append(k)
    if client is not None and getattr(client, "dry_run", None) != config.dry_run:
        client.dry_run = config.dry_run
        if "dry_run" not in changed:
            changed.append("dry_run")
    return changed
files.src/replay_engine.py:
"""Replay a recorded price tape through the REAL trading engine.

Reuses TradingEngine.check_targets (the live exit logic) with stub I/O — a tape-fed
quote provider, a no-op order client, and a capturing recorder. So a paper strategy's
new settings can be re-run over a trade's ACTUAL recorded price path, faithfully, with
ZERO duplicated logic (replaces the old hand-maintained simulate()).

Pure Python — no requests/network — so it also loads in Pyodide for in-browser replay.
"""
from datetime import datetime

from .config import Config            # noqa: F401  (re-exported for callers/Pyodide)
from .engine import TradingEngine
from .models import AlertType, ParsedAlert
from .positions import Position


def _dt(ts):
    """Parse a Supabase ISO timestamp to an aware datetime (None if unparseable)."""
    if not ts:
        return None
    try:
        return datetime.fromisoformat(str(ts).replace("Z", "+00:00"))
    except Exception:
        return None


class TapeQuotes:
    """Returns the CURRENT tape tick; the harness advances it each step."""
    def __init__(self):
        self._q = {"last": 0.0, "bid": 0.0, "ask": 0.0}

    def set(self, last, bid=None, ask=None):
        last = float(last)
        self._q = {"last": last,
                   "bid": float(bid) if bid is not None else last,
                   "ask": float(ask) if ask is not None else last}

    def quote(self, _sym): return dict(self._q)
    def last(self, _sym): return self._q["last"]
    def bid(self, _sym): return self._q["bid"]
    def ask(self, _sym): return self._q["ask"]
    def clear(self): pass


class NoOpClient:
    """Orders are no-ops; dry_run drives the engine's simulated-fill / stop-sim path."""
    dry_run = True

    def __init__(self): self._n = 0
    def place_order(self, _order, label=""): self._n += 1; return "REPLAY-%d" % self._n
    def cancel_order(self, _order_id): pass


class CaptureRecorder:
    """Captures the engine's event stream instead of writing to SQLite/Supabase.
    `ts` is set by the harness to the current tape tick, so each event carries the
    timestamp it fired on (drives the replay chart markers + the 'what happened' log)."""
    def __init__(self): self.events = []; self.ts = None

    def open_position(self, *a, **k): return 1
    def update_position(self, *a, **k): pass
    def log_sample(self, *a, **k): pass
    def log_tape(self, *a, **k): pass
    def log_flag(self, *a, **k): pass
    def log_alert(self, *a, **k): pass

    def log_event(self, position_id, etype, qty=None, price=None, note=""):
        self.events.append({"type": etype, "qty": qty,
                            "price": round(price, 2) if price is not None else None,
                            "note": note, "ts": self.ts})

    def close_position(self, position_id, exit_price, realized_pnl):
        self.events.append({"type": "_closed", "price": round(exit_price, 2),
                            "pnl": round(realized_pnl, 2), "ts": self.ts})


def replay_trade(config: Config, entry: dict, tape, alerts=None) -> dict:
    """Replay one trade's tape under `config`.

    entry:  {ticker, osi_symbol, right (Right enum), strike, qty, fill_price, opened_at?}
    tape:   iterable of (ts, last, bid, ask)
    alerts: optional list of the trade's recorded PARTIAL/CLOSE alerts
            [{ts, type ("PARTIAL"|"CLOSE")}, ...]. Fed to the engine at the matching
            tape tick so a follow-the-alerts strategy replays faithfully. The engine
            still respects ignore_exit_alerts, so a rules-only strategy ignores them.
    Returns {events, realized, peak_gain_pct, exit_price}.
    """
    quotes = TapeQuotes()
    rec = CaptureRecorder()
    eng = TradingEngine(config, NoOpClient(), quotes, recorder=rec)

    opened_dt = _dt(entry.get("opened_at"))
    pos = Position(ticker=entry["ticker"], osi_symbol=entry["osi_symbol"],
                   right=entry["right"], strike=float(entry["strike"]),
                   quantity=int(entry["qty"]), fill_price=float(entry["fill_price"]),
                   opened_at=opened_dt, high_water=float(entry["fill_price"]))
    pos.rec_id = 1
    eng.book.open(pos)
    rec.ts = entry.get("opened_at")          # entry stamped at the trade's open time
    rec.log_event(1, "entry", pos.quantity, pos.fill_price)

    # Replicate the entry-time resting stop (engine._on_entry does this).
    if config.stop_loss_pct is not None:
        eng._set_stop(pos, pos.fill_price * (1 - config.stop_loss_pct),
                      "-%d%%" % int(config.stop_loss_pct * 100))

    # Prepare the recorded exit alerts as (datetime, ParsedAlert), in time order.
    pending = []
    for a in (alerts or []):
        atype = {"PARTIAL": AlertType.PARTIAL, "CLOSE": AlertType.CLOSE}.get(a.get("type"))
        adt = _dt(a.get("ts"))
        if atype is None or adt is None:
            continue
        pending.append((adt, ParsedAlert(type=atype, raw=a.get("raw", ""), ticker=entry["ticker"],
                                          strike=float(entry["strike"]), right=entry["right"],
                                          confidence=1.0)))
    pending.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
    ai = 0
    mh = config.max_hold_minutes
    need_time = bool(pending) or bool(mh)

    peak = pos.fill_price
    last_px = pos.fill_price
    for ts, last, bid, ask in tape:
        last_px = float(last)
        peak = max(peak, last_px)
        quotes.set(last, bid, ask)
        rec.ts = ts                         # stamp any event this tick fires with the tape time
        tick_dt = _dt(ts) if need_time else None

        # 1) feed any recorded alerts that have come due by this tick (the trader's exits)
        while ai < len(pending) and tick_dt is not None and pending[ai][0] <= tick_dt:
            eng.handle_parsed(pending[ai][1])
            ai += 1
            if eng.book.get(pos.ticker) is None:
                break
        if eng.book.get(pos.ticker) is None:
            break

        # 2) the strategy's own rule exits (stop / breakeven / trail / take-profit / take-half)
        eng.check_targets()
        if eng.book.get(pos.ticker) is None:
            break

        # 3) max-hold backstop
        if mh and opened_dt is not None and tick_dt is not None \
                and (tick_dt - opened_dt).total_seconds() / 60.0 >= mh:
            eng._exit(pos, pos.quantity, reason="max_hold", note="%dm max-hold" % int(mh))
            break

    # Open at tape end -> close at the last bid (no data past the tape).
    if eng.book.get(pos.ticker) is not None:
        eng._exit(pos, pos.quantity, reason="tape-end")

    return {"events": rec.events, "realized": round(pos.realized, 2),
            "peak_gain_pct": round((peak / pos.fill_price - 1) * 100, 1),
            "exit_price": last_px}
